---
title: "Neyman-Pearson Hypothesis Testing Using the Iris Dataset"
author: "Wandera Paul Emmanuel"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output:
  html_document:
    toc: true
    toc_depth: 3
    number_sections: true
  pdf_document: default
  word_document: default
---

# Introduction

This seminar demonstrates the Neyman-Pearson approach to statistical hypothesis testing using the Iris dataset in R. The report covers the theoretical foundation, likelihood ratio tests, hypothesis formulation, implementation, visualization, and interpretation of results.

# Objectives

1. Explain the Neyman-Pearson Lemma.
2. Demonstrate likelihood ratio hypothesis testing.
3. Perform left-tailed, right-tailed, and two-sided hypothesis tests.
4. Interpret statistical results.
5. Illustrate hypothesis testing using the Iris dataset.

# Methods

The analyses are conducted in R using reproducible scripts contained in this repository.

# Results

Results generated by the analysis scripts will appear here.

# Discussion

Interpretation of findings will be provided here.

# Conclusion

Key findings and recommendations will be summarized here.

# References
